title: Market Brief — Monday, May 4, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while H200s at 45% are tightening and B200s at 25% border scarcity with GB200s at 10% in severe shortage. B200 utilization surged 30.9pp in 30 days to 90%, the biggest move in the stack, signaling next-gen demand is outrunning supply buildout.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while H200s at 45% are tightening and B200s at 25% border scarcity with GB200s at 10% in severe shortage. B200 utilization surged 30.9pp in 30 days to 90%, the biggest move in the stack, signaling next-gen demand is outrunning supply buildout.

DeepSeek is burning cash: V3.2 output at $0.378/M sits well below TCO-adjusted self-hosting cost on any GPU class, selling at an estimated loss of $4-5 per million tokens. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints 95%+ margins on B200s and is the most profitable endpoint in the market. Anthropic Haiku 4.5 at $5/M output lands in negative margin territory on A100s and H100s, losing roughly $4/M tokens after TCO adjustment.

B200 and GB200 compute is undervalued given utilization trends approaching saturation at current spot rates; locking forward capacity now is the trade. o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued relative to GPT-5.4 at $15/M given comparable capability tiers, and enterprise buyers should arbitrage down immediately.
bullets: 25% B200 availability with 90% utilization signals scarcity within 60 days.
10% GB200 availability marks a severe shortage before the chip has even scaled.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss of roughly $4.65/M against TCO-adjusted B200 cost.
96.7% H100 utilization rose 10.3pp in 30 days despite 70% nominal availability.
$80/M output on o3 Pro delivers 95% gross margin on B200 infrastructure.
21.41 LLMflation index means frontier output costs have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
